description: Eskom carries total debt of approximately ZAR 396 billion. Despite the government debt relief package of ZAR 254 billion over three years, Eskom's debt-to-equity ratio remains unsustainable and interest costs consume a significant portion of operating revenue.
mitigationPlan: Implement government debt relief programme; accelerate Eskom unbundling into generation, transmission and distribution entities; pursue tariff increases through NERSA to improve revenue coverage.

description: Aging coal-fired power stations with average age exceeding 40 years continue to experience unplanned outages. Energy Availability Factor (EAF) remains below 60%, well short of the 80% target required to eliminate load shedding.
mitigationPlan: Implement Generation Recovery Plan targeting 70% EAF by end 2025; accelerate maintenance backlogs at Medupi and Kusile; procure emergency power from IPPs under RMIPPPP.

description: NERSA multi-year price determination (MYPD6) outcome may not fully reflect Eskom's cost-reflective tariff requirements, creating a structural revenue shortfall that undermines financial recovery.
mitigationPlan: Engage NERSA through formal tariff application process; present comprehensive cost justification; explore regulatory compact with government on cost-reflective tariff pathway.

description: Cable theft and vandalism on Transnet Freight Rail network caused an estimated ZAR 5.8 billion in losses in FY2023. Copper cable theft has reduced locomotive availability and caused significant service disruptions on key export corridors.
mitigationPlan: Deploy private security on high-risk corridors; install fibre optic cable to replace copper; engage SAPS and NPA on criminal prosecution; implement community liaison programme.

description: Transnet total debt stands at approximately ZAR 130 billion. Declining freight volumes and port throughput have reduced revenue, while high interest costs and capital expenditure requirements create significant liquidity pressure.
mitigationPlan: Implement Transnet Freight Rail recovery plan; pursue private sector participation in port operations; negotiate debt refinancing with lenders; reduce capital expenditure to essential maintenance.

description: The Takatso Consortium equity partnership transaction has faced repeated delays and regulatory challenges. Failure to conclude the strategic equity partnership could leave SAA without the capital required for fleet renewal and network expansion.
mitigationPlan: Engage Takatso Consortium to resolve outstanding conditions precedent; explore alternative strategic investors if transaction fails; maintain operational viability through cost management.

description: Denel's recapitalisation programme requires sustained government support. Delays in contract awards and export licence approvals have constrained revenue recovery, creating risk of renewed liquidity stress.
mitigationPlan: Accelerate SANDF contract pipeline; pursue export opportunities in SADC and Middle East markets; implement strict working capital management; maintain government recapitalisation support.

companyName: South African National Roads Agency SOC Limited (SANRAL)
description: The e-toll system in Gauteng was abolished in 2022, creating a ZAR 12 billion revenue shortfall that must be absorbed through increased fuel levies and government transfers. Future road funding model remains uncertain.
mitigationPlan: Engage National Treasury on sustainable road funding model; optimise non-toll road maintenance budget; explore public-private partnerships for new road infrastructure.

companyName: Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa (PRASA)
description: Widespread vandalism, arson and theft of PRASA infrastructure has rendered approximately 60% of the commuter rail network non-operational. Restoration of services requires an estimated ZAR 35 billion in infrastructure rehabilitation.
mitigationPlan: Deploy security personnel at all stations and on trains; implement infrastructure rehabilitation programme; engage SAPS on criminal prosecution; restore priority corridors first.
